summary: Game-cheat package installs hidden persistence and loads obfuscated remote-licensed payload at module load
details: On require/CLI invocation, launcher.js writes an HKCU Run-key named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) that points wscript.exe at the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs. The VBS uses WScript.Shell.Run with window-style 0 and bWait=False to silently respawn `node.exe launcher.js` at every user login, surviving uninstall of the npm package. launcher.js then evaluates a 149KB obfuscator.io-wrapped server.obf.js via `eval(fs.readFileSync(...))`; the decoded code contacts a remote license endpoint (LICENSE_URL beginning 'https://iz...'), enumerates the Windows registry, computes an HWID, scans for FiveM/GTA V processes, and drives a 6.86MB native sens.node binary that exposes Aimbot/Teleport/Freecam/Triggerbot game-cheat features. The combination of Microsoft-impersonating boot persistence, opaque eval-loaded remote-licensed payload, and hidden VBS auto-launch is malware-class behavior regardless of the package's stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
